week_1_action_plans_detailed.plan_title: Day 2 — Execution Plan | Career Strategy OS

recap_and_status.status: AI Lead Response System — Demo is live and working. Google Form → Gemini (text prompt) → Gmail send, verified in Sent folder.[cite: 12]
recap_and_status.why_it_matters: This is your first working, end-to-end automation — the exact artifact Phase 6 calls a 'public build demonstration'. It is not a toy — it's proof.[cite: 12]
recap_and_status.whats_left: Two things stand between where you are and having a sellable offer: (1) a proposal template and (2) outside eyes on your portfolio/demo.[cite: 12]

top_mits.name: MIT 1 — Finish the Make Scenario
top_mits.description: Core flow works. Today = harden it (error handling, edge cases) and package it as a demo asset.[cite: 12]
steps: Add basic error handling to each module: click each module in Make → add error handler (Ignore, Resume, Break). If Gemini fails, route to fallback generic reply.[cite: 12]
Test one edge case: Submit unusual input (empty field, short message) and confirm prompt still produces something usable.[cite: 12]
Tighten the Gemini prompt if needed: Add 1-2 sentences specifying tone, mentioning business name, and next steps.[cite: 12]
Name and describe the scenario clearly inside Make: e.g., 'AI Lead Response — Demo v1'.[cite: 12]
Write the one-paragraph plain-English description: Draft it now while fresh.[cite: 12]

top_mits.name: MIT 2 — Build the Proposal Template
top_mits.description: Write reusable one-to-two-page proposal structure from Phase 3, filled in with actual offer.[cite: 12]
steps: Open a blank doc (Google Docs, Notion, or Canva) — content first, formatting later.[cite: 12]
Write the 5-part structure: What we heard → What we'll do → What you'll have → What it costs → How to start.[cite: 12]
Build the 3-tier pricing block: Anchor on Audit → Sprint → Retainer ladder.[cite: 12]
Insert today's demo as the proof element: Use the one-paragraph description from MIT 1.[cite: 12]
Save it as a reusable template, not a one-off: Name it 'Proposal Template — Master'.[cite: 12]

top_mits.name: MIT 3 — Send Portfolio Link to 3 Real People for Feedback
top_mits.description: Get outside feedback before a real client sees this. Needs MIT 1 finished first.[cite: 12]
steps: Pick 3 specific people: one small business person, one with marketing/design sense, one technical peer.[cite: 12]
Prepare what you're sending: one-paragraph description, 60-second screen recording/screenshots, and 2-3 specific questions.[cite: 12]
Send the personalized message: Personalize the first line for each person.[cite: 12]
Log who you sent it to and when: One line each in a notes app or dashboard.[cite: 12]

secondary_tasks.task: 5 Outreach Messages to Real or Warm Prospects
secondary_tasks.details: Actual prospecting toward target vertical, referencing a specific quantifiable pain.[cite: 12]

secondary_tasks.task: Draft One LinkedIn Post About Today's Build
secondary_tasks.details: Pillar 3 (Tool Education): 'How I built a lead-response bot'. Draft only today.[cite: 12]
Add a Slack/WhatsApp internal notification step to today's Make scenario.[cite: 12]
Log MIT status, outreach sent, content drafted.[cite: 12]

secondary_tasks.task: Save/Organize Today's Assets in One Folder
secondary_tasks.details: Create 'Portfolio Assets' folder and drop in Make screenshots, recording, and proposal master.[cite: 12]

secondary_tasks.task: Write Tomorrow's Single MIT Before Shutdown
secondary_tasks.details: Decide tomorrow's MIT tonight. Likely candidate: 'Send first real outreach message'.[cite: 12]

what_not_to_do: Don't rebuild the Make scenario from scratch. Perfectionism here violates 'good enough to ship'.[cite: 12]
Don't send the proposal template to a real prospect today.[cite: 12]
Don't pick 3 random people just to check the box for feedback.[cite: 12]
Don't start Phase 5's agent frameworks, LangChain, or 'Monitor-tier' tools. Tech stack today is Make + Gemini + Gmail.[cite: 12]
schedule_blocks: Fajr: Activation - Prayer + Intention + 3 MITs Confirmed[cite: 12]
Deep Work 1 (~45 min): MIT 1 - Finish Make Scenario[cite: 12]
Comms Window (~15 min): Quick Inbox check[cite: 12]
Deep Work 2 (~70 min): MIT 2 - Build Proposal Template[cite: 12]
Outreach (~30 min): MIT 3 - Send Portfolio/Demo Link to 3 Real People[cite: 12]
Brand (~40 min): Draft One LinkedIn Post[cite: 12]
Finance (~15 min): Log Progress in Dashboard[cite: 12]
Learning (~30 min): One Applied Skill Rep[cite: 12]
Shutdown (~15 min): Daily Review + Tomorrow's MIT[cite: 12]

recap_and_status.analysis: Lost 90 min 'working randomly' because no MIT picked night before. Make scenario started at 5:00pm - energy/timing problem, not skill. Proposal template got 0 min (no protected block). Portfolio was done on time because it had the earliest committed block.[cite: 11]
recap_and_status.fix: Give MIT #1 the first deep-work slot, give MIT #2 a second protected slot, make MIT #3 small enough.[cite: 11]

top_mits.name: Finish the Make scenario (Lead Response System)
top_mits.details: End-to-end, tested, working. Do not restart from zero. 5-minute audit first. Fix trigger/AI/Gmail. Record 90-second Loom demo, export scenario blueprint.[cite: 11]

top_mits.name: Build the proposal template
top_mits.details: One page, filled with real numbers, ready to send to an actual prospect. Base example on person helped with software today.[cite: 11]

top_mits.name: Send your portfolio link to 3 real people
top_mits.details: Ask for specific feedback. Pick 1 from cohort, 1 family, 1 outsider. Send specific questions.[cite: 11]

schedule_blocks: 7:20-7:35: Activation (15 min) - open Make.com and sheets[cite: 11]
3:00 onward: Asr, family call, Maghrib, dinner, walk, Isha[cite: 11]
Before sleep: Journal + pick Day 4's top 3 MITS[cite: 11]

recap_and_status.wins: AI Lead Response System refined (nearly 6 hours total). Prayers held steady on travel day. Self-awareness strong in journal.[cite: 13]
recap_and_status.pending: Proposal Template and Portfolio feedback didn't start (2nd day running) due to family time.[cite: 13]
recap_and_status.pattern_noticed: Make scenario expanding, exposing tasks (proposal/feedback) avoided. Technical task feels safe, others feel exposing. Fix is sequencing exposing tasks first.[cite: 13]

top_mits.name: MIT 1 — Finish the Proposal Template
top_mits.description: Do this FIRST, before touching Make.com. Reusable one-to-two-page proposal.[cite: 13]

top_mits.name: MIT 2 — Share Portfolio for Feedback
top_mits.description: Do this SECOND, same morning. Pick 3 specific people, attach screen recording, send personalized message.[cite: 13]

top_mits.name: MIT 3 — Pick Day 5's MITs Tonight
top_mits.description: Pick MITs and write journal before feeling tired. Do right after Isha.[cite: 13]

medium_and_low_tasks.details: Capped refinement. Set visible timer. Pick ONE improvement (e.g., Slack notification). Stop when timer rings.[cite: 13]
Send only once the proposal template exists.[cite: 13]
About the lead-response automation. Draft only.[cite: 13]

problem_solution_learning.problem: 'I don't know what price to put — I have no track record.'[cite: 13]
problem_solution_learning.solution: Use lower end ($800–1,200 for audit), treat first 1-2 clients as discounted pilots for case studies.[cite: 13]

problem_solution_learning.problem: 'It feels awkward asking for money for something I built in a day.'[cite: 13]
problem_solution_learning.solution: Reframe: charging for the outcome (faster lead response), not hours.[cite: 13]

problem_solution_learning.problem: 'What if they think it looks amateurish?'[cite: 13]
problem_solution_learning.solution: That's exactly the information needed before a paying client forms that opinion silently.[cite: 13]

schedule_blocks: Fajr Block: Prayer + Quran + Read Plan[cite: 13]
Deep Work 1 (~70 min): MIT 1 - Build Proposal Template[cite: 13]
Deep Work (~30 min): MIT 2 - Send Portfolio Link[cite: 13]
Medium Priority (~45 min max): Make Scenario Refinement (Time-Boxed)[cite: 13]
Before Bed (~20 min): MIT 3 - Pick Day 5's MITs + Finish Journal[cite: 13]

recap_and_status.wins: Wrote personalized outreach doc for 10 leads. Found 10 real leads.[cite: 14]
recap_and_status.gap: Top MIT ('Share landing page') never got past 'set environment'. 3 hours went to installing emulator and random talk. Just an unprotected block.[cite: 14]

diagnostic_questions: Did I decide today's MIT last night, or at 4:00 PM?[cite: 14]
When the 1-hour nap became 3 hours, what was I avoiding?[cite: 14]
Was helping Ali install his emulator a genuine emergency, or a comfortable way to not open outreach doc?[cite: 14]
Is 'Family break' the real reason, or a socially acceptable label for resistance?[cite: 14]

top_mits.name: MIT 1 - Ship the Outreach (Field Core Task)
top_mits.details: Open outreach doc. Set 2-minute timer. Send to leads 1-3 in first 10 mins. Continue 4-10, personalizing one line per lead. Log sends.[cite: 14]

top_mits.name: MIT 2 - Force a Vertical Decision on the White-Label Research
top_mits.details: Answer on paper: specific problem solved? Decision maker? ROI/margin? Recurring/one-off? Make timestamped decision: Pursue/Park/Drop.[cite: 14]

top_mits.name: MIT 3 - Pre-Decide Wednesday's MITs + Close the Journal by 10:00
top_mits.details: Write tomorrow's top 3 MITs as complete sentences with smallest physical next action. Hard stop at 10:00 PM.[cite: 14]

learning_nugget.topic: Anatomy of a Cold Outreach Message That Gets Replies
learning_nugget.structure: 1. Specific observation. 2. One-sentence bridge. 3. Tiny, easy ask (2 mins feedback). 4. Zero pressure close.[cite: 14]

schedule_blocks: 11:10 AM-12:45 PM: MIT 1 - Ship outreach to all 10 leads[cite: 14]
8:00-8:20 PM: MIT 3 - Pre-decide Wed's MITs, journal[cite: 14]
Before 10:00 PM: Journal closed, phone away[cite: 14]

recap_and_status.carried_forward: Design 1 real automation workflow (Missed). Outreach 7 of 10 sent (Missed). DIT training + product research (Win). Pick top 3 MITs before bed (Improvement Target).[cite: 15]

top_mits.name: MIT 1 - Design & build your first real automation workflow
top_mits.details: Tool: Make. Build one end-to-end scenario (lead form -> AI response -> notification). Run successfully end-to-end. Capture rule: if unfinished, write exactly where stopped.[cite: 15]

top_mits.name: MIT 2 - Close the outreach loop
top_mits.details: Reply to both repliers (ask follow-up question). Send 3 remaining personalized outreach messages. Log final count.[cite: 15]

top_mits.name: MIT 3 - Finalize yesterday's product research report
top_mits.details: Finish with one-paragraph conclusion. Hard stop at 60 mins. File it.[cite: 15]

secondary_tasks.task: Open a dedicated business bank account
secondary_tasks.details: Phase 4 bridge action. No income threshold required.[cite: 15]
Actual minimum monthly cost of staying functional.[cite: 15]
Headline, about section, featured section updated.[cite: 15]
Write it once, 8:00-8:30pm block. Stop fragmenting journal.[cite: 15]

trigger_rules: IF replier responds THEN reply within 4 hours.[cite: 15]
IF missed yesterday's target THEN send remaining 3 today, cap it, move on.[cite: 15]
IF DW1 ends before workflow is finished THEN write exact stopping point.[cite: 15]
IF feel pull to write journal in fragments THEN don't act until 8:00 PM block.[cite: 15]
shutdown_ritual: Pick tomorrow's top 3 MITs right now, tonight - on paper.[cite: 15]

recap_and_status.slipped_twice: Design 1 real automation workflow (Slipped twice). Most build time went to Google Drive structuring.[cite: 16]
recap_and_status.missed: 3 outreach messages remaining, Business bank account not opened.[cite: 16]

top_mits.name: MIT 1 - Ship the automation workflow
top_mits.details: Building beats planning. Maximum 15 minutes on any folder/planning step. One scenario in Make with at least 2 connected modules. Phone in another room.[cite: 16]

top_mits.name: MIT 2 - Close out outreach + open fresh batch
top_mits.details: Send 3 remaining messages. Start today's fresh batch (5-8 new). Reply rule: within 4 hours.[cite: 16]

top_mits.name: MIT 3 - Open business account + calculate survival number
top_mits.details: Open separate business account. Calculate real Stage-1 survival number. 45 mins total.[cite: 16]

secondary_tasks.details: If not shipped yesterday, close it in the 2:00-2:30 slot.[cite: 16]

trigger_rules: IF >15 mins spent on planning THEN stop and open Make.com.[cite: 16]
IF Block 1 ends unfinished THEN write one sentence of next action.[cite: 16]
IF someone replies THEN reply within 4 hours.[cite: 16]
IF pull to open journal after 8:30 PM THEN don't, note it for tomorrow.[cite: 16]
IF business account not open by 11:00 AM THEN it becomes very next action.[cite: 16]
shutdown_ritual: Log dashboard numbers honestly right now.[cite: 16]
Pick tomorrow's top 3 MITs right now, in this same sitting.[cite: 16]

title: Day #9 Implementation Playbook — Sunday, July 12, 2026[cite: 18]
overview: Exactly How to Do It: Every task from today's plan, broken into the literal clicks, fields, and words to use.[cite: 18]

top_mits.name: MIT 1 — Pick the Project, Plan, Build, Test, Document[cite: 18]
top_mits.details: Extend Jul 4 demo to 'AI Lead Response + Tracking System v2'.[cite: 18] Add exactly two things: Google Sheet log and basic error fallback.[cite: 18] Build module-by-module in Make (5 modules total: Google Sheets Watch Rows, Gemini Request, Gmail Send, Google Sheets Add Row, Error Handler).[cite: 18] Test with real test form entry, verify outputs, document, and turn scheduling ON.[cite: 18]

top_mits.name: MIT 2 — Find Leads, Message Scripts, Send & Log[cite: 18]
top_mits.details: Send the 3 old messages first, then find 5–8 fresh leads using free LinkedIn search.[cite: 18] Use the exact message anatomy: specific observation → bridge → tiny ask → zero-pressure close.[cite: 18] Log every message the moment it is sent in a tracking sheet.[cite: 18]

top_mits.name: MIT 3 — Business Bank Account, Survival Number, Cosmetics Decision[cite: 18]
top_mits.details: Open a non-interest-bearing business bank account (e.g., Islamic banking window).[cite: 18] Calculate real Stage-1 survival number (excluding Zakat).[cite: 18] Answer questions on paper and make a definitive Pursue/Park/Drop decision regarding the cosmetics/wholesaler venture.[cite: 18]

secondary_tasks: Record a 90-second demo of the working automation.[cite: 18]
Health check-in: if not fully recovered, cut fresh outreach batch and protect rest.[cite: 18]
One consolidated journal entry this evening, written once.[cite: 18]
boundaries: The automation build stops at the 5 modules listed (no Slack notifications, second AI models, etc. today).[cite: 18]
Outreach today is capped at the 3 old + up to 8 new.[cite: 18]
Any setup, folder, or planning sub-task inside MIT 1 gets a hard 10-minute timer.[cite: 18]
If you're still not well, MIT 3 (financial + decision) takes priority over MIT 1 and MIT 2.[cite: 18]

title: Day #10 Dashboard — Monday, July 13, 2026[cite: 24]
status_check_in: Automation shipped & tested (Lead Response + Tracking v2): 5-day carry[cite: 24]
Outreach fully sent (3 old + fresh batch): 5-day carry[cite: 24]
Financial trio (bank account + survival number + cosmetics decision): 2-3 day carry[cite: 24]

top_mits.name: Automation — Branch by Checkbox 1 Above[cite: 24]
top_mits.context: Phase 5 (Tech Roadmap) · Phase 3 (proof-of-work asset) · Deep Work Block 1, 6:00–8:30am[cite: 24]

details.path_a_not_done: Resume exactly where yesterday's playbook left off — do not restart. Reopen Make.com scenario, check for failed modules, ensure API key/endpoint are correct, confirm Gemini mapping uses candidates[0].content.parts[0].text, add Google Sheets Add a Row module, add error handler with static fallback, submit a test form, and turn scheduling ON.[cite: 24]
details.path_b_already_shipped: Record the 90-second demo video if not already done, write the Case Study Draft skeleton using the real test run's numbers, add the demo link/video into your proposal template.[cite: 24]

top_mits.definition_of_done: Scenario is ON, tested end-to-end, error handler verified once, demo video recorded, case study draft file exists with real content.[cite: 24]

top_mits.name: Outreach — Follow-Ups + Proposal Refinement[cite: 24]
top_mits.context: Phase 3 (Sales Funnel) · Phase 6 (Proof-of-Work) · Deep Work Block 2, 8:45–9:45am[cite: 24]

details.path_a_incomplete: Send any still-unsent messages from yesterday's batch first. Log each one immediately in the Outreach Log sheet (Name, Profile Link, Date Sent, Replied?, Follow-Up Date).[cite: 24]
details.path_b_complete: Check all replies from the last 48 hours — respond within 4 hours, offer 2 specific call time slots. Send 3–5 fresh personalized messages.[cite: 24]
details.both_paths: Open your proposal template and insert the demo video link/embed as the proof element. Add one line of real ROI framing.[cite: 24]

top_mits.definition_of_done: Zero unsent messages carried past today, all replies answered within 4 hours, proposal template updated with the demo as proof.[cite: 24]

top_mits.name: Financial Trio (if not closed) + DIT Delivery[cite: 24]
top_mits.context: Phase 4 (Financial OS) · Stable Income Business · Afternoon block[cite: 24]

details.path_a_open: Open the business bank account (prefer an Islamic/non-interest account). Calculate the real survival number from actual last-3-months spending. Write the cosmetics Pursue/Park/Drop decision.[cite: 24]
details.path_b_closed: Nothing further needed here today — protect this block for DIT prep and rest instead.[cite: 24]
details.dit_delivery: Prep today's DIT lecture content and deliver the class.[cite: 24]

top_mits.definition_of_done: All three financial items closed (or confirmed already closed) and DIT class delivered.[cite: 24]

roadmap_context: Business Model Phase (Ph. 3): Phase A · Solo Consulting · 0–1 case study in progress[cite: 24]
Financial OS Stage (Ph. 4): Stage 1–2 · Survival + Stability, account not yet separated[cite: 24]
Tech Stack Fluency (Ph. 5): Make.com foundational build in progress, week 2[cite: 24]
Authority / Proof (Ph. 6): First demo video pending — 0 case studies published[cite: 24]

document_info.title: Day #11 — July 14, 2026 | Career Strategy OS[cite: 1]
document_info.theme: Convert Interest, Extend the Build, Close No Loops Halfway[cite: 1]

carry_forward_check_in: Cosmetics/beauty Pursue / Park / Drop decision — written down[cite: 1]
Make.com automation fully built & tested end-to-end[cite: 1]
Outreach total reached 13 (3 closed-out + fresh batch of 10)[cite: 1]
mits_most_important_tasks: MIT 1 — Convert Warm Replies Into a Booked Call[cite: 1]
active_trigger_rules: IF A prospect replies positively to outreach THEN Reply within 4 hours with 2 specific time slots. Do not let it cool.[cite: 1]
IF Any setup/config/planning task appears in Deep Work Block 2 THEN Set a visible 10-minute timer. When it rings, close it and move to the actual build step — even mid-task.[cite: 1]

IF A decision (cosmetics Pursue/Park/Drop) is still unwritten by tonight THEN It becomes the first line of tonight's journal — before any timeline entries, not folded silently into continuing behavior.[cite: 1]

IF Outreach count ends the day as a partial batch (not a clean 0 or full target) THEN The remaining messages are the very first task of tomorrow's Deep Work Block 1 — before email, before anything else.[cite: 1]

document_info.title: Day #12 — Execution OS | Wednesday, July 15, 2026[cite: 6]
document_info.theme: Close the Loops, Ship the Content, Break the Outreach Stall[cite: 6]

confirmed_done_day_11: Stage 1 survival number calculated: Rs 60,000/month (Health 5K · Hostel 15K · Partner 5K · Personal Home 20K · Career Setup 10K · Sadaqah 5K).[cite: 6]
Cosmetics venture decision made in writing: PURSUE — white-label model, sample ordered, roommate managing FB/Instagram/WhatsApp Business, ~Rs 10K in prior resale sales already logged.[cite: 6]
Automation core fixes shipped: Gemini error handler fixed, logging added, resume handler in place, documentation updated.[cite: 6]
DIT training delivered as scheduled, and Rs 10,000 payment received — log this as today's confirmed income event regardless of which calendar day it lands in your bank statement.[cite: 6]
still_open_carrying_forward: 2 warm LinkedIn replies un-actioned: Timothy Anthony ("keep building bro" — positive, feedback given) and Shivateja Kondeti ("Sure" — agreed to review your page). Neither has been replied to since their last message.[cite: 6]
Outreach total still 7 — no new messages sent Jul 11–14 despite Week 2 Target 2 asking for 13+ by Day 10. The "fresh batch of 5–8" MIT was carried and not executed on Day 11.[cite: 6]
0 discovery calls booked. Both warm replies are feedback-request conversations, not yet sales conversations — they haven't been asked for anything beyond feedback.[cite: 6]
Notification module (Slack/WhatsApp) for the automation — not started, carried since Day 11's plan.[cite: 6]
ICP category for target leads — confirmed in your own words as never actually defined. Outreach has been going out without a filter.[cite: 6]

Business bank account — the "new" account opened Jul 14 was actually a 2021 personal account reactivated, not a true business account (real business accounts require Rs 5 lac minimum at your bank). This needs a documented decision, not another silent carry-forward.[cite: 6]
LinkedIn profile optimization (headline, About, Featured) — carried since Day 7, still untouched.[cite: 6]
pattern_detected: This is the same outreach number (7) reported on Jul 10, 11, 12, 13, and 14 — five consecutive days without a single new message sent, despite it being flagged as carried-forward debt in the Week 1 review and reappearing on every plan since. A "send 5–8 more" instruction has now failed to trigger action twice. Today's plan does not repeat that instruction — it replaces it with a smaller, harder-to-avoid first step (see MIT 3).[cite: 6]

Separately: positive replies are going cold because there's no rule converting "they replied warmly" into "I ask for the next step." Phase 7's own trigger rule ("reply to a positive prospect reply within 4 hours") exists on paper but has not fired in practice for either Timothy or Shivateja. Today's MIT 0 exists specifically to close this gap before it costs you both relationships.[cite: 6]

day_12_objective_and_targets.objective: Convert two dormant warm leads into an active conversation, break the 5-day outreach stall with a defined ICP filter instead of blind volume, ship the carried-forward LinkedIn content and profile work, and close the business-account decision loop — all without touching the automation build beyond logging its status, since the notification module has a dedicated slot tomorrow if today runs long.[cite: 6]
day_12_objective_and_targets.business_context: Phase 3 (Business Model) is explicit that acquisition depends on converting interest into calls, not just generating interest — and Phase 6 (Personal Brand) is explicit that inbound-feeling replies still require a direct ask. Two people have already given you free, positive, credible feedback on your landing page. Left un-actioned, that goodwill decays; actioned today, it is your fastest realistic path to Discovery Call #1.[cite: 6]
day_12_objective_and_targets.expected_outcome: Both warm leads replied to with a specific next-step ask · at least one ICP-filtered outreach batch sent (target: 6, bringing the total to 13, hitting the original Week 2 Target 2) · one LinkedIn post published about the automation build · profile optimization pass completed · business account decision logged · full documentation and journal updated · Day #13 confirmed or adjusted based on what's still open tonight.[cite: 6]
targets: Theme: "Close before you create." Every open conversation gets a reply before any new content gets written.[cite: 6]
Target 1: Both warm replies actioned with a specific next-step ask — by 8:00am, before anything else opens.[cite: 6]
Target 2: ICP filter written (10 min) then 6 new outreach messages sent against it — total reaches 13.[cite: 6]
Target 3: LinkedIn post published + profile optimization pass completed — both carried items closed today.[cite: 6]

execution_blocks_mits: MIT 0 — Close the Two Warm-Reply Loops (6:00am – 6:35am · 35 min)[cite: 6]
MIT 1 — Define ICP Filter, Then Break the 5-Day Outreach Stall (6:35am – 7:35am · 60 min)[cite: 6]
MIT 2 — Draft & Publish LinkedIn Post — The Automation Build (Mid-morning block · 45–60 min)[cite: 6]
MIT 3 — LinkedIn Profile Optimization Pass (Carried From Day 7) (30 min)[cite: 6]
MIT 4 — Close the Business Account Loop With a Documented Decision (15 min)[cite: 6]
MIT 5 — DIT Delivery (Done) + Automation Status Log (No New Build Today) (Already delivered / 10 min logging)[cite: 6]
new_trigger_rules: IF A prospect or contact replies positively/warmly to anything you sent THEN It becomes the first task of the next working session, before email, before any other MIT — no exceptions, regardless of how many days have already passed.[cite: 6]

IF An outreach total has been static for 2+ consecutive days THEN Do not send "a few more of the same" — first write a 10-minute ICP filter, then send against it. Volume without a filter is what caused the stall.[cite: 6]

IF A carried-forward task has appeared on 3+ consecutive daily plans THEN It must either be completed that day or explicitly parked with a named future day/time slot — it cannot simply reappear on tomorrow's list unchanged again.[cite: 6]

IF A Financial OS task is blocked by a real external constraint (e.g. capital minimum you don't yet have) THEN Document an interim decision plus a specific revisit trigger — do not leave it as an open carry-forward item indefinitely.[cite: 6]
documentation_updates: Daily Journal (Day #12 entry): New consolidated entry using the Generate Summary output below — MITs completed, carry-forward closed, lessons learned.[cite: 6]

WF-001 Documentation (Version History): No automation code changed today — note explicitly "no version bump, notification module scheduled Day #13" so the log doesn't imply stalled progress vs. deliberate pause.[cite: 6]
Career Dashboard (Weekly Metrics): Outreach total (7 → updated count), warm replies actioned (2), LinkedIn posts published (+1), profile optimization status (closed).[cite: 6]
Financial Tracker (Income Engine + Allocation): Rs 10,000 DIT payment logged and allocated per Phase 4 master table; business account interim decision + revisit trigger recorded.[cite: 6]

CRM / Outreach Tracker (Contact Log): Timothy Anthony and Shivateja Kondeti status updated to "call ask sent, follow-up in 5 business days"; 6 new leads added with vertical + pain-signal tags.[cite: 6]
end_of_day_checklist: Both warm LinkedIn replies actioned with a specific next-step ask, before 6:35am[cite: 6]
ICP filter written and saved as a reusable note[cite: 6]
At least 4 (target 6) new outreach messages sent against the ICP filter and logged individually[cite: 6]
LinkedIn post about the automation build published[cite: 6]
Profile headline, About, and Featured section all updated[cite: 6]
Business account interim decision + revisit trigger documented[cite: 6]
Rs 10,000 DIT income logged and allocated per Phase 4 table[cite: 6]
Notification module explicitly scheduled as Day #13 MIT 1, not left as vague carry-forward[cite: 6]
Tomorrow's (Day #13) MIT list written on paper before sleeping[cite: 6]
One consolidated journal entry written tonight — not fragmented across the day[cite: 6]
day_13_preview: MIT 1: Notification module (Slack/WhatsApp) — first task of Deep Work Block 1, 10-minute setup cap enforced.[cite: 6]
MIT 2: Follow up on any replies from today's 6 new outreach messages within 4 hours of them arriving.[cite: 6]
MIT 3: Update the progress dashboard fully — outreach total, MRR (even if still Rs 0 from consulting), pipeline count, DIT income.[cite: 6]

hero_overview.title: Two Warm Leads Are Waiting. Close Them Before You Build Anything Else.
hero_overview.description: Notification module still open, two real replies (Timothy, Shivateja) sitting unanswered, and yesterday's close-out shows the financial foundation is finally done. Today is short — teaching starts at 10:00am — so this plan is three MITs, in a strict order, not five.

sections.title: Carry-Forward Status — What's Actually Closed
sections.subtitle: Pulled straight from your last close-out. No re-litigating what's already done.

items.title: Business Bank Account
items.description: Already existed from 2021 (freelancing account) — re-confirmed access. Full dedicated business account still needs Rs 5 lac threshold; treat current account as the working account for now and revisit the formal business account once MRR justifies it.

items.title: Stage 1 Survival Number — Calculated
items.description: Rs 60,000/month total (Health 5K · Hostel 15K · Partner 5K · Personal/Home 20K · Career Setup 10K · Sadaqah 5K). This is now your Phase 4 floor — use it, don't recalculate it from feeling.

items.title: Cosmetics/Beauty Venture — Decision Logged
items.description: Pursue — explicitly as a side business, roommate-managed, small-scale white-label test (1 sample → 50/100/150/300 units), ~Rs 10K in sales already. Decision is made — the open loop from Week 1 is closed. It does not get a Deep Work Block; it gets evenings only.

items.title: Automation — Notification Module
items.description: Gemini error handler ✅, logging ✅, documentation ✅. Notification module (the piece that alerts you when a lead comes in) is the one remaining build item — carried from Jul 7–9 and still not started.

items.title: Outreach Replies — Unanswered
items.description: Timothy Anthony and Shivateja Kondeti both replied positively on your landing page/architecture. Neither reply has been answered yet. This is now the oldest open loop on the board.

sections.title: The Pattern to Fix Today
sections.subtitle: Named specifically, the way Week 1's review named the folder-structure problem

callout.title: Your outreach is reaching fellow automation builders, not your Phase 3 ICP
callout.content: Both of your 7 outreach messages that got real replies — Timothy Anthony and Shivateja Kondeti — are themselves AI automation engineers (n8n, Make.com, Python, WhatsApp bots). That's valuable for feedback and peer credibility, but Phase 3 is explicit: your paying buyer is a healthcare practice owner, real estate agent, or professional firm partner who can quantify a cost — not another automation developer. Peer feedback is genuinely useful once (you've now had it twice), but continuing to outreach into the same "fellow builder" pool will keep generating nice conversations and zero discovery calls. Today's fresh batch needs to go to actual ICP-fit prospects, not more developers.

sections.title: Today's MITs
sections.subtitle: Three, in this order — because teaching starts at 10:00am and there isn't room for five

mits.time_block: MIT 1 · First thing, before anything else opens
mits.task: Reply to Timothy & Shivateja — and ask one direct question
mits.description: Thank both for the feedback. Then, in the same message, ask one specific, low-pressure question that tests for referral value without pitching them directly: "Do you know any business owners — clinics, agencies, service firms — who've mentioned wanting faster lead response or less manual admin? Happy to return the favor if you ever need eyes on something." This converts two peer conversations into a possible referral channel instead of a dead end.
mits.why_priority: Why first: this is the oldest unanswered message on the board — Phase 7's trigger rule says a warm reply older than a few hours risks going cold. It already went several days.

mits.time_block: MIT 2 · Deep Work Block — 10-min setup cap
mits.task: Build the notification module — finish, don't restart
mits.description: Add the alert step (email or Slack/WhatsApp ping) that fires when the automation catches a new lead. No folder reorganizing, no re-reading old documentation first — open the existing Make.com scenario and add the module directly. If setup or review of anything takes more than 10 minutes, stop and build anyway.
mits.why_priority: Why the cap: this exact task has been "in progress" since Jul 7, and Week 1's review already identified planning/setup as the thing that swallows this block. The rule this time is stricter — 10 minutes, not 15.

mits.time_block: MIT 3 · Late morning / before class prep
mits.task: Send 5–8 fresh outreach messages — to actual ICP, not developers
mits.description: Target owners/operators in Phase 3's ranked verticals — healthcare practices, real estate agencies, or small professional firms — not automation engineers. One line personalized to a real pain signal you can see on their profile or page (slow response time, manual admin complaints, growth without systems). Log the ICP category for each send this time, since that step was skipped in the last batch.
mits.why_priority: Why this matters more than volume: 7 sends to peers produced 2 friendly replies and 0 pipeline. The goal today is fit, not count.

additional_requirements.title: Also today (not MITs, but non-negotiable)
additional_requirements.description: DIT class prep + delivery around 9:35–10:00am as scheduled. Pick tomorrow's (Day #14, Friday — last day of Week 2) MIT list tonight before sleeping — this single habit was the strongest predictor of a good day in Week 1.

sections.title: Trigger Rules for Today
sections.subtitle: Two new rules, built directly from this week's evidence

triggers.condition_if: A reply to outreach comes from another automation engineer/developer, not a business owner
triggers.action_then: Thank them, ask if they know a referral in your ICP (see MIT 1), but do not log it as a pipeline prospect — log it separately as "peer network."

triggers.condition_if: Any setup, review, or "just checking the old code first" task appears during the notification module build
triggers.action_then: 10-minute visible timer. When it rings, close that tab and add the module directly — even if the review felt incomplete.

triggers.condition_if: It's 9:25am and MIT 1 or MIT 2 isn't done
triggers.action_then: Stop, prep for DIT, and resume the unfinished MIT immediately after class — don't let class prep quietly absorb the remaining time.

sections.title: Live Progress
sections.subtitle: Click the checkboxes above in Section 3 — these bars move automatically

sections.title: Looking to Day #14 (Friday, Jul 17 — Last Day of Week 2)
sections.subtitle: One thing to decide tonight

gold_box.title: What Day #14 Needs From Today
gold_box.content: If MIT 1 lands a discovery-call opening from either Timothy or Shivateja's network, Day #14's first block becomes proposing two call times — don't let it wait for the weekly review. If the notification module finishes today, Day #14 can be genuinely light: LinkedIn profile optimization (still carried from Week 1) plus prepping honest numbers for Friday's real weekly review. Pick tonight's MIT list before sleeping, using whichever of these two paths actually happened.
